The sun's setting later these days.#$b#Gives me a bit more time out on the water before I have to call it quits.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Mon4: Fall's settlin' in.$h#$b#Water temps drop this time of year. Changes which fish come to the surface.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Mon6: Cool air off the water today.$h#$b#Fall's my second favorite season for fishing. The fish start bulking up before winter. Makes 'em bold.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Mon8: I was out early in the fog this mornin'.$h#$b#Low fog on the water in fall makes a person feel like they're the only one in the world.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Tue4: Fall's a generous season.#$b#The fish feed hard before winter. You don't even have to try as hard to get a bite.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Tue8: Walleye start runnin' when it rains in fall.#$b#Not many folk know that. Good tip if you want a rarer catch.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Wed2: Autumn fog on the water is something else.#$b#Peaceful. The kind of quiet that settles around you proper.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Wed6: The salmon are runnin' upstream now.#$b#Remarkable thing to watch. Worth seein' if you haven't.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Thu4: A lot of fishermen slow down when the season changes.$s#$b#Me, I reckon fall has its own rewards if you're willing to adapt.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Thu6: Getting darker earlier now.#$b#Long evenings by the dock. I don't mind it.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Fri4: Friday in fall. The town quiets down this time of year. Fine by me.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Fri8: Good week.$h#$b#The sea treated me well. At this point I feel like we understand each other.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Sat2: The docks are calmer now.$h#$b#I take my time in the mornings. There's no rush when you've been at it this long.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Sat6: Sun sets earlier now.$h#$b#I watch it from the dock when I can. Makes a person grateful for what they've got.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Sun2: Simple Sunday.$h#$b#Just me and the sound of the tide. Best kind there is.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Sun6: The sea's never the same color twice.$h#$b#I notice things like that, after all these years. Pays to pay attention.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Mon: The chill is starting to set in.#$b#Water gets colder, fishing gets a bit tougher.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Tue2: I prefer the quiet of autumn.#$b#Fewer folks around, more time to just listen to the waves.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Tue6: You ever notice how the sea smells different in the fall? Crisper, somehow.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Wed4: Make sure you're stocking up for winter.#$b#It creeps up faster than you expect here.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Wed8: I usually spend these cooler evenings going over my charts...#$b#And sorting tackle.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Thu2: The wind's got a bite to it today.#$b#A good hot bowl of soup sounds about right for dinner.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Thu8: I enjoy the change of pace.#$b#Summer's a busy time, but fall lets a person breathe.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Sat4: Some of the fish are getting restless.#$b#Preparing for the freeze, I reckon.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Sat8: It's peaceful out by the water today.#$b#Makes all the hard work worth it, you know?
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fall_Sun4: I've been thinking about getting some new wood for the stove.#$b#Needs to be dry before the snow hits.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Winter_Mon: Cold's settled in proper.$h#$b#The fish don't disappear in winter. They just go deeper. You have to know where to find 'em.
I've been keepin' the fire in the shop goin' all week.$h#$b#Old building. Needs the warmth. So does this old man, if I'm honest.
Ice fishing is its own thing entirely.#$b#Slower. Quieter. Takes a different kind of patience than open water fishing.
The sea looks heavier in winter.#$b#Like it's working harder. I respect that.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Winter_Tue: Not many folk venture out in this cold.#$b#Suits me fine. Fewer boats means cleaner water.
Been patchin' up gear all morning.Winter's a good time for maintenance. Long as the fire's going, I don't mind the cold.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Winter_Wed: Cold mornings by the docks have a strange beauty to 'em.#$b#Takes some getting used to, but I wouldn't trade it.
Winter fishing's not for everyone.$h#$b#There's a kind of fish that only shows in the cold, though. Worth the effort if you can stand it.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Winter_Thu: Brisk this morning.$h#$e#The cold bothers me a bit more than it used to.$s#$b#But the fish don't wait for old aches, so neither do I.
Winter's when I do most of my reading.$h#$b#Old books on the sea, mostly. Some of 'em go back generations.
The docks are quieter in winter.$h#$b#Peaceful, mostly. Sometimes it makes me think about the people and places I've seen from the water.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Winter_Fri: End of the week.#$b#I'll treat myself to something warm from Gus tonight. A small reward for a cold week.
Clear sky today.$h#$b#Cold, but the visibility out on the water is something special. You can see clear to the horizon.
I've been thinking about a story my father used to tell.$h#$b#Some of 'em I've carried for decades. Good stories don't go stale.
Glad you came by. Come inside where it's warmer if you like.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Winter_Sat: The best thing in winter is keeping busy.#$b#The sea doesn't take days off, and neither do I if I can help it.
Cold as you'd expect.$h#$b#The cod are running deep this week. Good for long-line fishing if you've got the patience.
The valley looks different under snow.$h#$b#Even the shoreline. Beautiful in its own right.
My best catch of the week came in this morning.$h#$b#Makes up for the cold weather. Always does.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Winter_Sun: A quiet Sunday in winter.#$b#Just the way I like it.
I'm grateful for this place.$h#$b#The sea, the valley, the folk around here. Good life.
Even in the coldest days, the sea's still out there.$h#$e#Consistent. That's something I've always respected about it.
Long winter evening.$h#$e#I'll sit by the dock a while yet before I head in. No rush.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Winter_Mon: The docks get slippery this time of year.#$b#Watch your step near the water.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Winter_Tue: I don't mind the cold...#$b#As long as the wind stays quiet.
You've got a lot of grit being out in this weather.#$b#Most folks stay by the fire.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Winter_Wed: The shop gets a bit drafty in the winter months.#$b#Just part of living by the sea.
I was just thinking about closing up shop early today.#$b#Hard to focus when your hands are numb.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Winter_Thu: Not much happening in town lately.#$b#Everyone's waiting for spring, I suppose.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Winter_Fri: Another cold week almost done.#$b#I'll try my luck out on the pier later if the ice holds off.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Winter_Sat: Sometimes I just come out here...#$b#To listen to the ice shifting against the pilings.
You must have a good reason to be out here, @.#$b#Or maybe you just appreciate the cold quiet like I do.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Winter_Sun: Sunday out here feels slower than the rest of the week.#$b#Gives a person time to think.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Saloon_Ent: Evenin', Gus!/Got a stool for an old man?/Ah, smell that evening air... and the grease.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_FishShop_E: Back to it. The sea's always waiting.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Resort_Ent: Ah, the Fern Islands... Brings back a lot of memories.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Resort_Lea: Time to head back to the mainland. Boat's waitin'.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Resort: The water here's warmer. Changes the fish entirely.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Resort_Sho: I've seen some strange creatures wash up on these shores...$s
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Resort_Bar: A cold drink on a hot island. Can't complain about that.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Resort_Wan: Me legs appreciate the warm sand.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_RejectBouq: Now, @... I'm flattered, truly. But my heart belongs to the sea.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_RejectMerm: A Mermaid's Pendant? Lord... that's a mighty gesture. But I'm too set in my ways for marriage. The sea's the only mistress I need.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_RejectMovi: A picture show? Nay, I can't leave the shop today.
Bah, I don't care much for those loud action pictures.
I've already been to the pictures this week. Nothin' beats the stars, anyway.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_Fair_Judge: A blue ribbon! Your grandpa would be beamin' with pride today, @.$h
Don't let it get ye down. A fisherman doesn't catch a lunker every cast.
I... well. That's certainly one way to get Mayor Lewis's attention.
Ye didn't bring anything to show? Well, next year, eh?
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_WinterStar: Ah, it's you! Happy Feast of the Winter Star. I've got somethin' for ye.
Picked it out meself. Hope it helps ye on the farm.
A gift for an old man? Why, thank you kindly.$h
A fine specimen! The sea's bounty is the best gift of all.$h
Oh... well. It's the thought that counts, eh?
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_AcceptBirt: A birthday gift? And a mighty fine one at that! Thank ye, @.$h
Well... I suppose birthdays come with all sorts of surprises.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_AcceptGift: Ye pulled this out of the water? Bah, we need to keep our shores clean.
Trash... I've hauled up enough of this in my time. I'll dispose of it for ye.
Driftwood? It's good for the fire, if nothin' else. Much obliged.
Now that's some fine tackle there, @! Much obliged.$h
A fisherman can always make use o' sturdy materials. Thank ye.
Fresh forage from the valley, eh? Much appreciated, @.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyDialogue_RejectItem: Bah, keep yer flowers for the ladies, @.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottDialogue_Saloon_E: Ah, a cozy evening among friends./Gus, could I have my usual, please?
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottDialogue_SeedShop: Pierre, do you happen to have any nice paper today?
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottDialogue_Resort_E: The Fern Islands... what a lovely place for a romance story.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottDialogue_Resort_L: Goodbye, warm island air. Pelican Town will feel quite chilly after this.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottDialogue_Resort: The breeze here smells of flowers and sea salt...
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottDialogue_Resort_S: The seashells here are beautiful. Very different from the ones back home.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottDialogue_Resort_B: A drink with a tiny umbrella? How charming.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottDialogue_Resort_W: The sound of the waves here is wonderfully calming.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottDialogue_RejectBo: I... cannot accept this. Some stories are meant to stay in the past.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottDialogue_RejectMe: You're asking me again, even after how things ended between us?
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottDialogue_RejectMo: I'm sorry, but I really should spend today writing.
That film doesn't seem very inspiring to me.
I've already gone to the movies this week, I'm afraid.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottDialogue_Fair_Jud: Wonderful work, @! Your display was truly impressive.$h
Don't feel too bad, @. Not everyone can see beauty the same way.$s
Well... that certainly caught Mayor Lewis's attention.
An empty display? That's quite a bold choice.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottDialogue_WinterSt: Ah, my friend. I picked out something special for you.
I hope it adds a little beauty to your farm life.
Oh, this is for me? That's very thoughtful.$h
A handmade good! You can really feel the care put into it.$h
Oh... well, it's the thought that matters.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottDialogue_AcceptBi: A birthday gift? You've truly made my day, @.$h
Oh dear... well, thank you for thinking of me.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottDialogue_AcceptGi: I'm... not quite sure what to do with this.
Trash? I usually prefer things a bit more refined.
Ah, a lovely bottle of wine. You spoil me, @.$h
This was made with real care and skill. Thank you.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottDialogue_RejectIt: I'm afraid I don't collect garbage.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyInkQuestLetter: Hey there,^^I've been meaning to waterproof my old navigation charts before the rainy season hits. Sea charts are hard to come by, and these ones have been with me for years.^^I heard Squid Ink does wonders for preserving old paper. If you happen to get your hands on some, I'd be real grateful if you brought it by.^^- Willy^^%item quest LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyInkQuest %%[#]Note from Willy
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyInkQuest: ItemDelivery/Ink & Lines/Willy wants to waterproof his old navigation charts with Squid Ink./Bring Willy 1 Squid Ink./Willy (O)814 1/-1/400/-1/true/Now that's what I needed!$h#$b#These old charts'll last another generation. Thank you kindly.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyMapleQuestLetter: How's it going,^^My old pipe's been tasting a bit rough, carbon build-up, I reckon. My father showed me a trick once, a good rinse with Maple Syrup and a slow dry-out, and it's good as new.^^Sweet stuff like that's hard to come by near the docks. If you've got any to spare on the farm, I'd be much obliged.^^- Willy^^%item quest LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyMapleQuest %%[#]Note from Willy
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyMapleQuest: ItemDelivery/Pappy's Pipe/Willy's old pipe needs seasoning with Maple Syrup, a trick his father taught him./Bring Willy 1 Maple Syrup./Willy (O)724 1/-1/350/-1/true/Ah, perfect.$h#$b#Pappy always swore by this method. First smoke after a maple rinse is something else entirely. Much obliged.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottCoffeeQuestLetter: Farmer,^^The chapter is right there. I can feel it. But the hour is late and my eyes are going and I have, through some failure of planning, run entirely out of coffee.^^If you have any to spare, I would be in your debt. Truly.^^- Elliott^^%item quest LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottCoffeeQuest %%[#]Urgent Note from Elliott
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottCoffeeQuest: ItemDelivery/The Perfect Page/Elliott is so close to finishing a chapter but has run out of coffee./Bring Elliott 1 Coffee./Elliott (O)395 1/-1/300/-1/true/You have saved an entire chapter, @.$h#$b#I am not being dramatic. Well... perhaps slightly. But truly, thank you.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottDaffodilQuestLett: I want to give Leah something. She has sat through more drafts than any person should, always kind about it, and I've never properly thanked her.^^I was thinking a Daffodil would be simple and cheerful. The sandy soil along the beach isn't right for them, so I thought I'd ask you.^^- Elliott^^%item quest LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottDaffodilQuest %%[#]Letter from Elliott
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottDaffodilQuest: ItemDelivery/A Gift for Leah/Elliott wants a Daffodil to give to Leah as a small token of appreciation./Bring Elliott 1 Daffodil./Elliott (O)18 1/-1/250/-1/true/Lovely. Simple and honest, exactly right.$h#$b#She'll appreciate the thought far more than anything extravagant. Thank you, @.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyNautilusQuestLetter: There's a gap on the shelf above my stove that's been bothering me lately. My father kept a Nautilus Shell up there when I was young. Fine shape to it. A reminder that the sea makes things more beautiful than anything a person could craft.^^If one turns up on your travels, bring it by. I'd be glad for it.^^- Willy^^%item quest LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyNautilusQuest %%[#]Note from Willy
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyNautilusQuest: ItemDelivery/The Shelf Above the Stove/Willy wants a Nautilus Shell to fill a gap on his shelf, like the one his father kept./Bring Willy 1 Nautilus Shell./Willy (O)392 1/-1/200/-1/true/That's the one.$h#$b#Same shape as I remember. Pappy would've approved. Thank you kindly.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyCrabQuestLetter: Rain's coming in today and I've been meaning to test a new crab trap design. The only way to know if a trap's worth using is to put a real one through it.^^If you've pulled a Crab from the pots lately and have one going spare, I'd appreciate it.^^- Willy^^%item quest LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyCrabQuest %%[#]Note from Willy
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyCrabQuest: ItemDelivery/Trap Test/Willy wants to test a new crab trap design and needs a Crab to do it properly./Bring Willy 1 Crab./Willy (O)717 1/-1/300/-1/true/Good size on it.$h#$b#Let's see what this trap's made of. Appreciate the help.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottPomegranateQuestL: I am writing a scene in which a character eats a pomegranate for the first time. I want to describe it properly, which means I need to eat one first.^^Gus doesn't carry them. The sandy soil near the beach isn't suited for them either. If you have one from the farm, I'd be grateful.^^- Elliott^^%item quest LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottPomegranateQuest %%[#]Letter from Elliott
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottPomegranateQuest: ItemDelivery/Research by Taste/Elliott is writing a scene and needs to experience a pomegranate firsthand before he can describe it./Bring Elliott 1 Pomegranate./Elliott (O)637 1/-1/400/-1/true/Tart... complex.$h#$b#There's something almost ancient about this fruit. I think I have my paragraph now. Thank you, @.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottAmethystQuestLett: I have been trying to find the right word for the color of deep water in winter. Indigo doesn't quite do it. Violet is too soft.^^The closest thing I can picture is an amethyst held up to winter light. If you have one from the mines, I'd be glad to borrow it while I write.^^- Elliott^^%item quest LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottAmethystQuest %%[#]Letter from Elliott
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottAmethystQuest: ItemDelivery/The Word for That Color/Elliott needs n Amethyst to find the right color word for the deep winter sea in his writing./Bring Elliott 1 Amethyst./Elliott (O)66 1/-1/450/-1/true/Perfect.$h#$b#That's the exact shade. I have the word now.#$b#Keep it. It's earned its place in the prose.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyCrabMonsterQuestLet: I've been tryin' to set a few crab pots near the mountain lake, but the local Rock Crabs are tearin' up me lines. If you're headin' into the mines, do me a favor and thin 'em out a bit.^^I'll make it worth yer while.^^ - Willy^^%item quest LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyCrabMonsterQuest %%[#]Letter from Willy
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyCrabMonsterQuest: Monster/Crustacean Control/Willy says Rock Crabs are damaging his traps near the mountain lake. Head into the mines and take care of them./0 of 5 Rock Crabs defeated./Rock_Crab 5 null false/-1/500/-1/false
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottGhostMonsterQuest: I find myself blocked on a gothic chapter of my new novel. I require firsthand accounts of the supernatural, such as the spirits dwelling deep within the mines.^^If you could dispatch a few Ghosts and return with the tale of your encounter, I would be grateful, and happy to compensate you.^^ - Elliott^^%item quest LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottGhostMonsterQuest %%[#]Letter from Elliott

Monster/Darker Inspiration/Elliott needs inspiration for a Gothic chapter and has asked you to slay some Ghosts in the mines while taking notes./0 of 8 Ghosts defeated./Ghost 8 null false/-1/1200/-1/false
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillySardineQuestLetter: Hey there,^^My father had a recipe for smoked sardines, salt-cured the old way, that I haven't made in years. Been thinkin' about it this year.^^Can you grab me a good lookin' sardine?^^- Willy^^%item quest LucyTheDove.PTE_WillySardineQuest %%[#]Note from Willy
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillySardineQuest: ItemDelivery/Old Recipe/Willy wants to make his father's smoked sardine recipe and needs a fresh Sardine./Bring Willy 1 Sardine./Willy (O)131 1/-1/150/-1/true/Ah, those are fresh.$h#$b#Pappy's recipe calls for salt and smoke, nothin' else.#$b#I'll have it going by tonight. Thank you, @.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottQuartzQuestLetter: Good morning,^^I woke early today and sat watching the light come through my window. There's a really beautiful quality to spring mornings that I find impossible to describe in the abstract.^^The closest thing I've found to hold it in place is quartz, the way it traps brightness without quite becoming it. I'd love a piece for my writing desk.^^- Elliott^^%item quest LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottQuartzQuest %%[#]Letter from Elliott
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottQuartzQuest: ItemDelivery/Light in Stone/Elliott wants a piece of Quartz to keep on his writing desk as a source of spring inspiration./Bring Elliott 1 Quartz./Elliott (O)80 1/-1/120/-1/true/Oh, look at that...$h#$b#The light moves right through it. It's exactly what I was after.#$b#I'll keep it beside my inkwell. Thank you, @.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottLobsterQuestLette: Dear @,^^I've been daydreaming about a lobster dinner for the better part of a week, with good wine. Can you get a really fresh Lobster for me?.^^Warmly,^Elliott^^%item quest LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottLobsterQuest %%[#]Letter from Elliott
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottLobsterQuest: ItemDelivery/Exceptional Evening/Elliott has been dreaming of a lobster dinner and would like a fresh one from the sea./Bring Elliott 1 Lobster./Elliott (O)715 1/-1/350/-1/true/Oh, this is magnificent!$h#$b#What a beauty. Tonight will be a proper occasion.$h#$b#Thank you, @. Truly.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyBlobfishQuestLetter: Ahoy there,^^I was thinkin' back to my time at the Night Market. The deep sea brings up things most folk never see in their whole lives. The Blobfish is a miserable lookin' creature, but it's a marvel of the deep ocean.^^If you're headin' down in the submarine this winter and manage to catch one, I'd pay good money to study it.^^- Willy^^%item quest LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyBlobfishQuest %%[#]Note from Willy
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyBlobfishQuest: ItemDelivery/Deep Sea Marvel/Willy wants to study a Blobfish from the Night Market submarine./Bring Willy 1 Blobfish./Willy (O)800 1/-1/1000/-1/true/Aye, that's a face only a mother or a fisherman could love.$h#$b#Thanks, @. This brings back memories.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottDuckFeatherQuestL: Dear @,^^I'm working on a rather delicate passage today, and I find my writing utensils lacking. A proper quill might be just the thing to put me in the right frame of mind.^^If your ducks happen to drop a nice, whole feather, would you bring it by the cabin?^^- Elliott^^%item quest LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottDuckFeatherQuest %%[#]Letter from Elliott
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottDuckFeatherQuest: ItemDelivery/The Right Quill/Elliott needs a Duck Feather to use as a quill for his writing./Bring Elliott 1 Duck Feather./Elliott (O)444 1/-1/400/-1/true/Oh, this is splendid.$h#$b#The weight of it, the way it balances... I think this will do nicely. Thank you, @.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillySturgeonQuestLetter: Greetings,^^The waters are warm enough now that the Sturgeon are making their way to the mountain lake. They're strong fighters, but I know you've got the arm for it.^^If you manage to wrestle one out of the lake, bring it by the shop.^^- Willy^^%item quest LucyTheDove.PTE_WillySturgeonQuest %%[#]Note from Willy
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillySturgeonQuest: ItemDelivery/Mountain Fighter/Willy wants you to catch a Sturgeon from the mountain lake./Bring Willy 1 Sturgeon./Willy (O)698 1/-1/600/-1/true/Now that's a fine catch.$h#$b#They put up quite a fight, don't they? You've got the makings of a master angler, @.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyOctopusQuest: ItemDelivery/The One That Surfaced/Willy spotted an Octopus off the coast during a storm and wants you to bring one in./Bring Willy 1 Octopus./Willy (O)149 1/-1/1000/-1/true/That's the one.$h#$b#Same deep red. I knew I wasn't imagining it.#$b#You've got good hands on you, @. And good patience.Thank you, truly.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyOctopusEvent_Line1: Close the door would ya, @. Knock the rain off those boots.$s#$b#...Somethin' happened out there this mornin'. Been on my mind all day.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyOctopusEvent_Line2: I saw an Octopus. Out past the breakers, just before the storm hit.$s#$b#Water was dark, chop comin' in hard. And there it was, right at the surface... caught the light for just a second.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyOctopusEvent_Line3: Fascinating creature. Haven't seen one in these waters in a good while, couldn't shake it.$s#$b#They don't come up just anytime. Always late evenin', sometimes after dark. And they sure don't mind weather like today.
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyOctopusEvent_Questi: My hands aren't what they used to be in a squall like this.$s#$b#But you... you've got steady hands. Reckon you could reel an Octopus in?$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyOctopusEvent_Prompt: Will you catch an Octopus for Willy?
LucyTheDove.PTE_WillyOctopusEvent_Respon: Aye... that's the spirit.$h#$b#Storm's only getting worse. Keep your footing out there, @. I'll be waiting.$h
I understand, the sea's angry today. No fish is worth your life.$s#$b#Head home before the squall breaks. We'll try another day.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottPearlQuest: ItemDelivery/The Ocean's Pearl/Elliott has been reading old sea tales and wants to see a real Pearl up close for inspiration./Bring Elliott 1 Pearl./Elliott (O)797 1/-1/2000/-1/true/A real Pearl... look at how it catches the light.$h#$b#It's even more beautiful than the books described. Thank you, @. This will keep me writing for weeks!$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottPearlEvent_Line1: Ah, @. Come in... sorry if I seem distracted.$s#$b#I've had my nose in these books all morning.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottPearlEvent_Line2: I'm feeling a bit stuck on my writing lately. It's like my mind keeps drifting out to sea...$u#$b#My current story just feels so flat. I really need a spark of inspiration.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottPearlEvent_Line3: I keep reading about old sea routes, and the legends of pirate ships looking for treasure.$s
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottPearlEvent_Line4: The books speak of the Pearl. A jewel born of the ocean's depths, holding the very essence of the sea itself.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottPearlEvent_Line5: I've always wondered if something so magical is real, or just a myth made up by lonely sailors.$s#$b#But the more I read, the more I believe it's out there.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottPearlEvent_Line6: If I could just hold one in my hands... if I could just see that magic for myself...$u#$b#I know it would finally cure this stubborn writer's block.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottPearlEvent_Questi: ...Which is why I have a huge favor to ask, @.$s#$b#I know the deep sea is dangerous, but you're braver than anyone else I know.#$b#Could you help me find one? A real Pearl from the ocean?
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottPearlEvent_Prompt: Will you help Elliott find a Pearl?
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottPearlEvent_Respon: Ah... a true adventurer!$h#$b#I can't thank you enough, @. Just the thought of finally seeing one gives me so many ideas.$h#$b#Take all the time you need. I know it won't be easy to find, but I believe in you.#$b#Please be careful out there!$h

O-oh... I understand, @. It was too much to ask.$s#$b#Diving into the deep sea is dangerous, I shouldn't have put that on you.$u#$b#Please forget I asked. I'll just have to find inspiration somewhere else.
LucyTheDove.PTE_Willy8HeartNight_Line2: Aye... you can watch the waters with me, @.$s
LucyTheDove.PTE_Willy8HeartNight_Line4: Water's getting cold tonight... Moon's bright though.$s
LucyTheDove.PTE_Willy8HeartNight_Line5: You know... nights like this, when the water goes flat like glass... I think about the year I first sailed into this valley.$s
LucyTheDove.PTE_Willy8HeartNight_Line6: It was a completely different kind of quiet back then. Most folks hadn't even heard of Pelican Town. But your grandfather... he was one of the first people to shake my hand on this very pier.
LucyTheDove.PTE_Willy8HeartNight_Line7: He wasn't a seafaring man, no. Got seasick just lookin' at a skiff. But he understood the pull of it.
LucyTheDove.PTE_Willy8HeartNight_Line8: We'd sit out here for hours, crackin' open a cold one, talkin' about the things that matter... and the things that don't.
LucyTheDove.PTE_Willy8HeartNight_Line8A: We used to cast lines right off this post. He had a terrible cast, always caught his hook on the old pier piling...
LucyTheDove.PTE_Willy8HeartNight_Line8B: But one night, he pulled up something strange. An old brass lockbox, heavy and covered in barnacles.
LucyTheDove.PTE_Willy8HeartNight_Line8C: He pried it open, took one look, and made me swear not to tell a soul what was inside. Said the valley wasn't ready to know... I reckon it's still buried somewhere on your farm.
LucyTheDove.PTE_Willy8HeartNight_Line8D: Hah! Guess your Gramps didn't want me runnin' my mouth about that!$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_Willy8HeartNight_Line9: This valley's changed a lot since those days. He passed on. Folks grew up. Old buildings got torn down and new ones sprung up.
LucyTheDove.PTE_Willy8HeartNight_Line10: But the sea... she stays exactly the same. She remembers everything we leave behind.$s
LucyTheDove.PTE_Willy8HeartNight_Line11: I'm glad you're here, @. Running that farm, keepin' his spirit alive.
LucyTheDove.PTE_Willy8HeartNight_Line12: He'd be properly proud of you.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottWritersBlock_Line: Oh! @. I didn't hear you approach. My mind was... elsewhere.$s
I was hoping the rhythm of the waves would clear the fog in my head, but... alas.
I look at my manuscript, and all I see are words. Hollow, lifeless words. They don't sing. They don't breathe.$s
Ah... the curse of the writer. To feel so much, yet be utterly unable to express it.
Tell me, @... when you look at your farm, at the life you've built... do you ever doubt if you are truly meant to do it?
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottWritersBlock_Ques: Do you ever doubt your life's path?
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottWritersBlock_Opti: Yes, I doubt myself all the time.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottWritersBlock_Resp: You do? That... strangely brings me comfort. To know that someone as capable as you also wrestles with uncertainty...$l
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottWritersBlock_Opti: Sometimes, but you just have to push through.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottWritersBlock_Resp: Pragmatic, as always. You're right, of course. A farm doesn't tend itself while you wait for inspiration... and neither does a book.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottWritersBlock_Opti: Never. I know exactly who I am.
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottWritersBlock_Resp: Oh... how enviable. I suppose some of us are just built with weaker constitutions.$s
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottWritersBlock_Line: You've given me much to think about, my friend. Perhaps the doubt itself is part of the process... the very thing that gives the work its soul.
I think... yes, I think I have a new scene in mind! I must return to my cabin at once!$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_ElliottWritersBlock_Outr: Thank you, @. You're a true friend to the arts.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_Mon: The spring air is crisp this morning.#$b#It's a good day to slow down and listen to the birds singing in the canopy.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_Tue: I found some fresh wild horseradish near the lake today.#$b#Spring is a good season for foraging.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_Wed: The mountain is waking up from winter.#$b#Every new green leaf is a reminder of how nature renews itself.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_Thu: Sometimes I sit by the lake and just watch the ripples.#$b#It's a simple pleasure, but it helps clear my mind.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_Fri: Do you spend much time among the trees, @?#$b#If you sit quietly, you can learn a lot from them.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_Sat: I don't need much to be happy.#$b#Just the warm sun and the soft grass under my feet.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_Sun: A quiet day today.#$b#I hope you find peace in your work on the farm, @.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_2: The morning air smells like cherry blossoms today#$b#It makes me want to spend the whole day walking in the forest.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_3: Winter was long, but the first green shoots are showing in the grass#$b#I found a tiny spring flower near a rock that I've protected with a ring of pebbles.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_4: The morning mist is lifting from the lake, leaving dew on all the grass.#$b#It's a beautiful sight to see before the town wakes up.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_5: The spring flowers are starting to bloom in the valley.#$b#It is nice to see the color return to the hills.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_6: A mother bird is building a nest in the bush near my tent#$b#I've been leaving out pieces of dry grass and moss for her.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_7: I saw a mother duck leading her ducklings down to the water today#$b#I sat quietly on a rock and watched them swim away.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_10: I saw a little rabbit nesting near my tent this morning#$b#It wasn't even scared as we shared a quiet moment together.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_12: The morning sun feels warm on my face today#$b#It makes me happy to sit by the tent and watch the birds.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_13: I found a patch of wild clover growing near the path today#$b#The bees were busy at work, and the whole area smelled sweet.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_14: The salmonberry bushes will be in season soon.#$b#It's a time of abundance, if you know where to look.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_15: I watched a hawk circle high above the mountain this morning#$b#It rode the warm air currents without even moving its wings.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_18: The ferns along the trail are uncurling their new fronds this week#$b#Each one looks like a tiny green hand slowly opening up to the sky.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_20: Spring is starting to fade into summer#$b#The forest changes its tune as the weather warms up.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_28: Tomorrow is summer#$b#The forest is green and full of life, which is always nice to see.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_Thu: Some days, the wind is a bit cold up here.#$b#You stopping by to chat makes the mountain feel much warmer, @.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_Fri: I've learned to appreciate the quiet of the mountain over the years.#$b#It's good to share a bit of that quiet with a friend like you.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Spring_Wed: I was just thinking about when I first met you#$b#You became a good friend who looks past my lifestyle.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Summer_2: The summer sun is warming up the mountain.#$b#The lake is perfect for swimming now, though I prefer the shade.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Summer_5: The warm breeze today is perfect for drying my firewood#$b#I've been stacking logs near the tent to prepare for later seasons.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Summer_8: There are dragonflies skimming over the lake surface today#$b#They dart around so quick, it's almost like watching tiny sparks of light.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Summer_10: A family of squirrels was playing on my tent roof this morning#$b#They were so loud, but watching them run around was very cute.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Summer_12: It gets hot up here in the afternoon#$b#I spent some time resting under a large oak tree to enjoy the peace.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Summer_13: I found a small tide pool near the edge of the lake today#$b#There were little water beetles swimming circles in it.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Summer_15: The wild raspberries near the ridge are ripening now#$b#I picked just a handful so there's plenty left for the birds too.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Summer_18: I found a sun-warmed stone that fits perfectly in my hand#$b#It's comforting to hold as a small gift from the mountain.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Summer_22: I saw a shooting star from the ridge last night#$b#I didn't make a wish, because I already have everything I need right here.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Summer_23: I found a wild sunflower growing near the rocks today#$b#I left it alone so the bees can enjoy its bright color.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Summer_27: Summer is coming to a close#$b#I need to secure my tent before the nights start getting too cold.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Summer_28: Summer is ending today#$b#I'll miss the warm lake swims, but fall brings its own quiet beauty.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Summer_Tue: The cicadas have been buzzing all morning#$b#It is always a sure sign of a good summer.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Summer_Wed: I watched the sunrise from the ridge this morning#$b#The sky looked like it was completely on fire.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Summer_Thu: The lake is calm today.#$b#If you're looking for fish, they seem to be staying near the deeper water to keep cool.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Summer_Fri: Summer berries are easy to find right now.#$b#I've gathered enough for a sweet meal tonight.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Summer_Sat: The summer nights are very loud with crickets.#$b#I find the sound comforting when I'm trying to sleep.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Summer_Mon: I spent the morning watching a family of ducks swim in the lake#$b#I'm glad to see them doing so well.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Fall_2: Fall is here.#$b#The leaves are turning yellow, and wild mushrooms are starting to sprout near the logs.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Fall_5: A friendly owl has been nesting in the tree branches right above me#$b#Its quiet hooting at night is very peaceful to listen to.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Fall_8: I found a cluster of chanterelle mushrooms growing near the oak roots today#$b#They were bright orange, almost glowing against the dark soil.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Fall_10: I found a tiny hedgehog sleeping under some dry leaves today#$b#I covered it back up gently so it stays warm.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Fall_12: A friendly squirrel followed me for a bit today#$b#I patted its little head and thanked it for the company.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Fall_13: The spiderwebs this morning were covered in tiny drops of dew#$b#They caught the light like strings of little pearls.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Fall_15: The days are getting shorter now#$b#I've been gathering firewood to prepare for the cold before it arrives.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Fall_18: The squirrels have been busy gathering acorns today#$b#I left a few hazelnuts out for them to find.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Fall_22: The leaves are falling like rain today#$b#It's a beautiful sight, and walking through them makes a nice rustling sound.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Fall_23: A family of deer walked right past my tent this morning#$b#They moved so quietly through the mist, it was a beautiful sight.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Fall_28: Tonight is the last night of fall#$b#I hope all your crops are safely harvested before winter arrives tomorrow, @.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Fall_Tuesd: The air is starting to smell like dry leaves.#$b#Autumn is a peaceful time, but it moves very quickly.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Fall_Wedne: I saw some wild hazelnuts sprouting by the path.#$b#Nature is generous if you take the time to look around.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Fall_Thurs: It's getting cooler in the mornings#$b#I spent some time patching my tent to keep the draft out.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Fall_Frida: The mountain looks beautiful with the changing leaves.#$b#I hope you're taking time to enjoy the views, @.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Fall_Satur: The wind is starting to howl through the trees.#$b#It's a reminder that the seasons are shifting again.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Fall_Monda: I found some wild plums that were perfectly ripe today.#$b#If I find enough tomorrow, I'll save a few in a basket for you, @.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Fall_Wedne: I collected some beautiful red autumn leaves today#$b#I placed them around my tent to make it feel a bit more festive.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Fall_Fri6: The townspeople often look at me with suspicion#$b#You've always treated me with respect and I won't forget that.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Winter_1: Winter has arrived today#$b#The heavy frost means I must spend the day keeping the fire going.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Winter_2: The first snow of the year has settled on the pine branches#$b#It looks like the trees are wearing soft white coats.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Winter_3: The frost has made beautiful patterns on the stones today#$b#They look like little ice flowers, which is a nice winter treat.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Winter_5: The mountain lake is freezing over with thin ice today#$b#I have to be careful when gathering fresh water.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Winter_8: I spotted a little wren hopping between the frozen branches today#$b#Such a tiny bird, but it didn't seem bothered by the cold at all.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Winter_10: A small bluebird landed on my shoulder today#$b#It must have thought I was just another tree in the forest.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Winter_12: I saw a little rabbit hiding in the snow near the bushes today#$b#I threw a few wild berries near it, and it ate them right up.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Winter_13: The wind carved little ripples into the snow last night#$b#When I woke up, the whole hillside looked like a frozen ocean.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Winter_15: It's very quiet in the winter.#$b#Sometimes the silence is nice, but it can get a bit lonely when the frost sets in.$s
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Winter_22: The winter nights are very long and cold#$b#I like to look up at the bright stars, which keep me company when the wind is loud.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Winter_23: I brewed some warm blackberry tea over my fire tonight#$b#Holding the hot cup makes the cold wind much easier to ignore.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Winter_24: Tomorrow is the Feast of the Winter Star.#$b#I don't usually join the festivities, but I hope you have a nice time with the community.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Winter_25: I can hear the distant music from the village festival today#$b#Normally I would spend this day alone in the quiet winter snow.#$b#Having you come all the way up the mountain just to check on me... it makes my heart feel warmer than any hearth fire, @.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Winter_Tue: Even when the snow covers everything, the mountain has a quiet beauty.#$b#I like to watch the snowflakes gently dance through the branches.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Winter_Wed: The forest animals are mostly sleeping now.#$b#I make sure to tread lightly so I don't wake them from their dreams.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Winter_Thu: I found a tiny pinecone that had fallen in the snow today.#$b#It's a small reminder that new life is waiting for the spring.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Winter_Fri: I brewed some warm tea over my campfire today#$b#Holding the warm cup makes the cold wind much easier to ignore.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Winter_Sat: I saw some deer tracks leading down to the frozen lake.#$b#It is comforting to know we aren't the only ones finding our way through the cold.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Winter_Fri: Winter is tough, but a friendly face makes it much easier to bear.#$b#Thank you for checking on me, @.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Fall_Thurs: I had money once. A real income, a place in town, all of it.#$b#But the more I had, the less I liked what I saw in myself.#$b#So I walked away. I've never once looked back.$u
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Summer_Wed: Willy and I sailed out to the Fern Islands together when we were much younger.#$b#It's been a real long time...$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusDialogue_Winter_Sun: Willy has been my closest friend for more years than I can count.#$b#He never asks me why I live the way I do.#$b#It means a lot to me.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusSalmonberryQuest: ItemDelivery/A Taste of Spring/Linus spotted a big cluster of salmonberries up on the mountain but couldn't quite reach them. He asked if you could bring him one./Bring Linus 1 Salmonberry./Linus (O)296 1/0/200/-1/true/These are perfect. I spotted a big cluster up on the rocks last week but couldn't get to them. Thank you, @.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusFiddleheadQuest: ItemDelivery/Pressed Fern/Linus wants to press a fiddlehead fern to keep as a memento of the summer. He says they only look right when they're freshly picked./Bring Linus 1 Fiddlehead Fern./Linus (O)259 1/0/250/-1/true/Ah, this one is beautiful. I like to press them flat between two stones and keep them through the winter. Thank you, @.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusChanterelleQuest: ItemDelivery/The Good Mushroom/Linus has been searching the oak roots near his camp for chanterelles, but the good ones keep eluding him. He asked if you could bring him one./Bring Linus 1 Chanterelle./Linus (O)281 1/0/300/-1/true/Now that's a fine one. These are my favourite to find in the fall. I'll cook it over the fire tonight. Thank you, @.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusHazelnutQuest: ItemDelivery/For the Squirrels/Linus likes to leave hazelnuts out for the squirrels near his tent each fall. He's run short and asked if you could bring him one./Bring Linus 1 Hazelnut./Linus (O)408 1/0/150/-1/true/The squirrels will find this by morning, I'm sure of it. They always do. Thank you for helping me look after them, @.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusWinterRootQuest: ItemDelivery/Something Hearty/Winter roots are buried deep under the frozen ground and hard to dig up in the cold. Linus asked if you could bring him one to add to his stew./Bring Linus 1 Winter Root./Linus (O)412 1/0/200/-1/true/Winter roots make a simple stew go a long way. Thank you, @. This will keep me warm tonight.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusSalmonberryQuestLet: Hello, @.^^I spotted a big cluster of salmonberries up near the higher rocks this week, but I just couldn't reach them. If you happen to come across one on your travels, I'd be grateful if you could bring it by.^^- Linus%item quest {{ModId}}_LinusSalmonberryQuest %%
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusFiddleheadQuestLett: Hello, @.^^Every summer I like to press a fiddlehead fern between two flat stones. It's a small thing, but it helps me remember the season through the cold months.^^If you find one while you're out, would you bring it to me? They need to be fresh.^^- Linus%item quest {{ModId}}_LinusFiddleheadQuest %%
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusChanterelleQuestLet: Hello, @.^^The chanterelles are out near the oak roots this time of year. I've been looking, but the good ones seem to be hiding from me.^^If you spot one on your walk, I'd be very glad to have it.^^- Linus%item quest {{ModId}}_LinusChanterelleQuest %%
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusHazelnutQuestLetter: Hello, @.^^Each fall I leave a hazelnut near my tent for the squirrels. They've come to expect it, and I'd hate to disappoint them. I've run short this year.^^Would you mind bringing me one if you find some?^^- Linus%item quest {{ModId}}_LinusHazelnutQuest %%
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusWinterRootQuestLett: Hello, @.^^The ground is frozen solid up here and the winter roots are buried deep. My hands get stiff in the cold and digging is hard work.^^If you manage to find one, would you bring it by? A winter root stew goes a long way.^^- Linus%item quest {{ModId}}_LinusWinterRootQuest %%
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusLeekQuest: ItemDelivery/The First Green/Wild leeks are one of the very first things to push through the soil each spring. Linus likes to mark the beginning of the season with a simple leek broth and asked if you could bring him one./Bring Linus 1 Wild Leek./Linus (O)20 1/0/150/-1/true/There it is. That earthy smell is spring, plain and simple. I'll have this in a broth over the fire by tonight. Thank you, @.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusCaveCarrotQuest: ItemDelivery/Roots in the Dark/Cave carrots grow in the cool underground where most people never think to look. Linus ran low on his dried root supply and asked if you could bring him one from your mining trips./Bring Linus 1 Cave Carrot./Linus (O)78 1/0/200/-1/true/I dry these on a rack near the campfire and they keep for weeks. A very dependable food source if you know where to find them. Thank you, @.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusSpiceberryQuest: ItemDelivery/A Little Heat/Spice berries ripen early in the summer heat. Linus uses them to add flavour to otherwise plain foraged meals and asked if you could bring him one before they dry out on the vine./Bring Linus 1 Spice Berry./Linus (O)396 1/0/175/-1/true/Just what I needed. A little heat goes a long way when you're eating simple food. Thank you for the trouble, @.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusPurpleMushroomQuest: ItemDelivery/The Rare One/Purple mushrooms only appear deep in the mountain caves, and only late in the fall. Linus has spotted the right conditions but can't make the climb. He asked if you might find one on your next venture underground./Bring Linus 1 Purple Mushroom./Linus (O)422 1/0/400/-1/true/I rarely come across this one. The colour alone is something. I'll keep it where I can see it for a while before I cook it. Thank you, @.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusCrystalFruitQuest: ItemDelivery/Frozen Beauty/Crystal fruit only forms in the coldest weeks of winter, in places where the frost settles and doesn't lift. Linus has long been fascinated by them and asked if you could bring one by when you find one./Bring Linus 1 Crystal Fruit./Linus (O)414 1/0/350/-1/true/Look at how the light passes through it. The world finds ways to make beauty even in the harshest cold. Thank you, @.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusLeekQuestLetter: Hello, @.^^The wild leeks are starting to show along the lower slopes. I've made a habit of having leek broth on the first morning they appear each spring. It's a small ritual, but it matters to me.^^Would you bring me one if you find it on your walk?^^- Linus%item quest {{ModId}}_LinusLeekQuest %%
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusCaveCarrotQuestLett: Hello, @.^^I've been running low on my dried cave carrot supply. They keep well once dried and make a reliable meal when foraged food is thin.^^If you happen to come across one in the mines, I would be grateful if you could bring it by.^^- Linus%item quest {{ModId}}_LinusCaveCarrotQuest %%
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusSpiceberryQuestLett: Hello, @.^^The spice berries are coming in this week. I use a couple of them to liven up my foraged meals through the summer, and they don't stay on the vine long once the heat sets in.^^If you find one on your travels, would you bring it by before they dry out?^^- Linus%item quest {{ModId}}_LinusSpiceberryQuest %%
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusPurpleMushroomQuest: Hello, @.^^The conditions are right for purple mushrooms to be growing deep in the mountain caves right now. I've been watching for them, but the climb down into the lower levels is difficult for me this time of year.^^If you venture underground and find one, I would really appreciate you bringing it by.^^- Linus%item quest {{ModId}}_LinusPurpleMushroomQuest %%
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusCrystalFruitQuestLe: Hello, @.^^Crystal fruit only grows in the coldest weeks, in places where the frost holds deep and still. I've always found something remarkable about the way it forms.^^If you come across one in your winter walks, would you bring it by? I won't keep you long.^^- Linus%item quest {{ModId}}_LinusCrystalFruitQuest %%
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusHorseradishQuest: ItemDelivery/Something Sharp/The wild horseradish is one of the first things to sprout each spring. Linus uses a bit of it to add some heat to his meals and asked if you could bring him one./Bring Linus 1 Wild Horseradish./Linus (O)16 1/0/150/-1/true/Sharp and earthy. That's exactly what I needed to liven up a plain meal. Thank you, @.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusGrapeQuest: ItemDelivery/Wild Grapes/Wild grapes grow along the upper ridge in late summer. Linus spotted a cluster ripening but the path up was steep. He asked if you could grab one for him./Bring Linus 1 Grape./Linus (O)398 1/0/200/-1/true/Sweet and a little tart. I could taste the summer in it. Thank you for making the climb, @.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusBlackberryQuest: ItemDelivery/The Last of Summer/Blackberries ripen just as the air starts to turn cold. Linus likes to eat them while they last and asked if you could bring him one before the season moves on./Bring Linus 1 Blackberry./Linus (O)410 1/0/150/-1/true/Perfect timing. A few more weeks and they'll all be gone. Thank you, @.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusRedMushroomQuest: ItemDelivery/A Rare Find/Red mushrooms are harder to come by than the common ones. Linus has been keeping an eye on a hollow log up the slope and asked if you might find one before the season ends./Bring Linus 1 Red Mushroom./Linus (O)420 1/0/300/-1/true/I rarely come across these. I'll dry it carefully and make it last. Thank you, @.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusSnowYamQuest: ItemDelivery/Buried Deep/Snow yams are buried well under the frozen soil and difficult to dig out in the cold. Linus asked if you could bring him one to add some heartiness to his winter meals./Bring Linus 1 Snow Yam./Linus (O)416 1/0/200/-1/true/These take some effort to find in the frost. I'm grateful you went to the trouble. Thank you, @.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusHorseradishQuestLet: Hello, @.^^The wild horseradish is sprouting up near the lower path again. I like to use a little of it to add some warmth to my meals early in the season.^^If you happen to spot one while you're out, would you bring it by?^^- Linus%item quest {{ModId}}_LinusHorseradishQuest %%
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusGrapeQuestLetter: Hello, @.^^There's a cluster of wild grapes ripening up on the ridge. I spotted them a few days ago, but the path up is steep and my knees aren't what they used to be.^^If you're up that way, could you grab one for me?^^- Linus%item quest {{ModId}}_LinusGrapeQuest %%
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusBlackberryQuestLett: Hello, @.^^The blackberries are ripening along the mountain path. They won't last long once the cold sets in properly.^^If you find one on your walk, I'd be glad to have it before the season passes.^^- Linus%item quest {{ModId}}_LinusBlackberryQuest %%
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusRedMushroomQuestLet: Hello, @.^^I've been keeping watch on a hollow log up the slope where red mushrooms sometimes grow. I haven't managed to get up there to check on them this week.^^If you come across one, would you bring it by? They're harder to find than the common ones.^^- Linus%item quest {{ModId}}_LinusRedMushroomQuest %%
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusSnowYamQuestLetter: Hello, @.^^Snow yams grow deep under the frozen ground and take real effort to dig out when it's this cold. My hands cramp up quickly in the frost these days.^^If you find one while you're out, I'd be very grateful if you could bring it by.^^- Linus%item quest {{ModId}}_LinusSnowYamQuest %%
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusTentVisit_1: Oh! @. You caught me reminiscing.#$b#When you get to be my age, the mind tends to wander back to the old days.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusTentVisit_2: Long ago... before I pitched my tent here... my old friend Willy and I sailed out to the Fern Islands.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusTentVisit_3: We were young, chasing the horizon. We sailed through storms that would break a lesser vessel... just to see what was beyond the fog.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusTentVisit_4: We spent months living off the land on Ginger Island. The air was thick with the scent of wild spices and damp earth.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusTentVisit_5: Willy learned the ways of the ocean there. And I... I learned how to listen to the island. How to survive without leaving a scar on the land.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusTentVisit_6: It was a different life. But some things from those islands never really leave you.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusTentVisit_7: I didn't bring back gold or souvenirs... but I did bring back one living thing.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusTentVisit_8: I've nurtured it ever since. Waiting for the right place to plant it. But I think... my roots are already settled here. And this belongs to someone who is still building their home.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusTentVisit_9: A cherry sapling from the Fern Islands.#$b#Plant it on your farm. Let it grow tall, so the wind can always play a tune through its leaves.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusTentVisit_End: It means a lot to share these memories with you, @.#$b#Take good care of that sapling.$h
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusTentVisit_6Heart_1: Oh! @! You gave me a fright.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusTentVisit_6Heart_2: You are in a playful mood today, aren't you?
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusTentVisit_6Heart_3: I was just trying to concentrate on crafting a new fishing lure. People throw away so many perfectly good bottle caps near the lake.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusTentVisit_6Heart_4: Most people just see trash... but I see an opportunity.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusTentVisit_6Heart_5: It takes a certain kind of vision to see the value in what others simply discard.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusTentVisit_6Heart_6: I think you have that vision too, @. I've seen you out there scavenging and building.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusTentVisit_6Heart_7: I've never demanded that anyone else should conform to my lifestyle... but it brings me comfort to know I'm not the only one who respects the wild.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusTentVisit_6Heart_8: Here. I made this earlier from some fresh foraging. It will give you the energy you need for your own scavenging.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusTentVisit_6Heart_9: Keep your eyes open out there. The world is full of hidden treasures, if you learn to listen to its tune.
LucyTheDove.PTE_LinusTentVisit_6Heart_En: You are always welcome in my tent, @.#$b#Just try not to sneak up on me next time.$h
